slides.title: Functions That Adapt
slides.body: A function that always does the same thing is useful. A function you can **customize each time you call it** is powerful. That's what **arguments** are for.
slides.tip: Think of a function like a coffee machine — you choose the beans, water, and cup size each time you press the button.

slides.title: Parameters vs Arguments
slides.body: A **parameter** is the placeholder variable in `def`. An **argument** is the real value you pass when calling. Same concept, two different moments.
slides.code:
def greet(name):   # 'name' is the PARAMETER
    print("Hello,", name)

greet("Alice")     # 'Alice' is the ARGUMENT

slides.title: Positional vs Keyword Args
slides.body: Pass values **in order** (positional) or **by name** (keyword). Keyword args use `param=value` syntax and let you skip worrying about order.
slides.code:
def describe_pet(animal, name):
    print(name, "is a", animal)

describe_pet("dog", "Rex")              # positional
describe_pet(name="Luna", animal="rabbit")  # keyword
slides.output:
Rex is a dog
Luna is a rabbit
slides.tip: Keyword arguments make code self-documenting — you can see exactly what each value means.

slides.title: Default Parameter Values
slides.body: Give a parameter a **default value** with `=` in the `def` line. Callers can skip that argument entirely, or override it when they need something different.
slides.code:
def greet(name, greeting="Hello"):
    print(greeting + ",", name + "!")

greet("Alice")               # uses default
greet("Bob", greeting="Hey") # overrides it

slides.title: The Mutable Default Trap
slides.body: Never use a **list or dict as a default value** — Python creates it once and shares it across every call. Use `None` instead and create a fresh list inside the function.
slides.tip: This is one of Python's sneakiest gotchas — even experienced devs get bitten by it.

slides.title: You've Got the Controls
slides.body: You now know how to pass data into functions using **positional** and **keyword** arguments, set **defaults**, and avoid the mutable default trap. Functions just got a whole lot more flexible.
slides.tip: Rule to remember: positional arguments must always come before keyword arguments in a call.
